steps.description: Prepare mixtures of silica (S-50 or A-200) and potato starch with molar ratio SiO2/starch = 1:0.3. Pre-grind for 15 min at 500 rpm for homogenization of the mixture.

materials.name: Sipernat 50 (porous silica gel) or Aerosil 200 (fumed silica)

steps.description: Grind the mixture for 30 min (2 x 15 min) at 750 rpm to heat the system, resulting in starch gelation and better homogenization of the sample.

Pyrolysis of the mechanochemically prepared silica/starch mixture in a rotary quartz reactor under nitrogen atmosphere. Heat from 20 to 500 °C at 10 °C/min, then isothermal annealing at 500 °C for 3 h, followed by cooling to room temperature.

equipment.name: Rotary quartz reactor
equipment.settings: N2 atmosphere, 120 cm3/min, heating rate 10 °C/min, isothermal at 500 °C for 3 h

synthesis.notes: Carbosils without Ni are denoted SSNi-0 (Sipernat-based) and ASNi-0 (Aerosil-based). The molar ratio of SiO2/starch is 1:0.3. Mechanochemical treatment causes starch gelation and homogeneous deposition of carbon matter on the silica matrix. The carbon deposit content in the final carbosils ranges from 11 to 19 %.
